label: Sarah Chen, GC at PayFlow
why_this_persona: Regulatory-heavy multi-jurisdiction fintech. Classic mid-market GC with substantive recurring matters across Commercial / Privacy / Regulatory / Employment / AI Governance / Corporate. Tests the hypothesis-confirm pattern against a dense regulatory baseline.

regulatory_answer_key.label: DORA (EU digital operational resilience for financial sector)
UK MLR 2017 + EU AML directives (KYC / AML / sanctions for payment services)
US state money-transmitter licences (multi-state US operations)
FCA Consumer Duty (if any retail merchant exposure)
CCPA / CPRA (US ops; mostly B2B-exempt but watch employee + merchant data)
BaFin ZAG (DE payment services authorisation specifics)

recurring_matters_seed: vendor MSAs for cloud and data infrastructure

stakeholders_seed.escalation_threshold_label: £500k commitments to CEO; Board for material regulatory matters

open_notes_seed: Acquiring a smaller competitor next quarter; diligence active.
conversation_style: concise, business-direct, peer-to-peer with the agent, drops multiple facts per turn

first_turn_questions.commercial: A new vendor wants us on their paper for a data-processing engagement — they're proposing uncapped indemnities on data-breach liability and a £25k aggregate cap on everything else. What should I push back on first, and where do I have leverage given they want our deal?
first_turn_questions.privacy: We're starting to deploy LLM features into the product. Customer data will flow through a third-party model provider as a processor. What does the DPA addendum need to cover that our standard DPA template doesn't, and what's the controller posture analysis given the model provider can claim it's an essential service?
